question.text: Which committee was set up by SEBI in November 1996 to develop a regulatory framework for derivatives trading in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI set up the L.C. Gupta Committee on November 18, 1996 to develop a regulatory framework for derivatives trading in India. The committee submitted its report on March 17, 1998. **The Trap:** The J.R. Varma Committee was a different committee set up in June 1998 to recommend risk containment measures for derivatives. Students often confuse these two committees. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII specifically tests knowledge of these two committees and their distinct roles. **Key Takeaway:** L.C. Gupta Committee (Nov 1996) = regulatory framework. J.R. Varma Committee (June 1998) = risk containment measures. Remember: "G comes before V" — Gupta came first, Varma came second.
